evidence_quotes.quote: "live service" games with end of life (which they do not disclose on purchase)
the buyer is left unable to open, play or even access the product they have bought.

paragraphs.text: The EU has made same great advances in consumer protection in the past decade. Yet video game companies have been left unregulated for many years and have now began to sell "live service" games with end of life (which they do not disclose on purchase). At the end of life of a such game the buyer is left unable to open, play or even access the product they have bought. I honestly think this is unacceptable and I see no reason that game publishers, which sell hundreds of millions of euros worth of products on the EU market are left to basically do as they please. This comment is made in connection with the "stop killing video games" initiative which can be integrated in some way in this act. I do hope that the EU will take action on this matter.
